description_long: This is a cylindrical wooden barrel with a flat top and bottom, featuring two dark metal bands encircling its body for structural reinforcement. The wood grain is visible, giving it a natural, slightly weathered appearance, and there are some dark stains and scuff marks on the top and sides, suggesting outdoor or industrial use. One side of the barrel displays a prominent yellow warning label with a black skull and crossbones symbol, indicating hazardous or toxic contents. The barrel lacks any visible handles or spouts, and the top appears to be a solid, sealed surface, implying it is not currently open. The overall construction is robust, with the metal hoops providing additional strength to the wooden staves. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban or industrial environments, possibly used for storage or containment of dangerous materials.
description: A sturdy wooden barrel with metal bands and a yellow hazard label featuring a skull and crossbones. The surface is weathered and stained, indicating industrial or outdoor use.
description_view_0: The barrel is shown with a partial view of the yellow hazard label and visible wood grain, with both metal bands clearly seen.
description_view_1: This view shows the back side of the barrel, with no visible labels, just the wood texture and metal bands.
description_view_2: The side of the barrel is visible here, showing only the wood grain and metal bands, with no markings or labels.
description_view_3: The front of the barrel is shown, prominently displaying the yellow hazard label with a skull and crossbones, along with the wood texture and metal bands.
attribute_labeling_or_markings: yellow hazard label with skull and crossbones
